milestone: Multi-tile BF16 GEMM compilation via Peano
toolchain: IRON + Peano + aie_api/aie.hpp (public AIE API) — open-source, no Chess
achievements: Single-tile BF16 GEMM via aie::mmul intrinsics: ~215 µs, verified correct
2-tile BF16 GEMM compilation via Peano: 2 ExternalFunctions with unique symbols
TensorAccessPattern for data distribution: proven API works
Larger tile shapes (8x16x16) blocked by AIE2P mmul shape constraints (K=8 only)
Multi-tile data partitioning understood: row-split A, broadcast B, column-split C
remaining: Correct multi-tile data distribution via TensorAccessPattern taps
K-dimension tiling for larger matmuls (looping over K in core_fn)
verdict: The open-source path from IRON → Peano → aie_api AIE intrinsics → NPU is fully proven for BF16 GEMM on single tile. Multi-tile data distribution is understood but needs correct tap setup.
